key_insight: In alternately twisted tetralayer MoTe2, the valley Chern number of the first K-valley moiré band can be switched between +1 and -1 by layer sliding and an applied gate field.
engineering_relevance: This shows a practical route to electrically and mechanically tune moiré band topology for topological electronic devices and quantum Hall platforms.
method: Large-scale DFT calculations of ATT MoTe2 moiré bands
Continuum modeling of sliding- and gate-dependent moiré potentials
Track Chern number evolution under interlayer sliding and electric field
evidence_signal: The abstract reports calculated topological evolution of the first K-valley moiré band from +1 to -1 with sliding, and back to +1 with gating.
watch_item: The switching depends on precise control of stacking registry and electric field, which may be difficult to reproduce uniformly in real multilayer devices.
paper_title: Switching Chern number by sliding and gating in alternately twisted tetralayer MoTe2
